requirement: The stack assigns one enterprise patient identifier and returns the same identity when the same synthetic patient registers at any facility.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=patient identity. Register the same synthetic person at two hospitals and two clinics using exact and near-match demographics; verify one enterprise identity is returned and every match decision is logged.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: An authorized user can merge confirmed duplicate patient records and reverse an erroneous merge without losing source identifiers, provenance, or clinical data.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=patient identity. Create two records for one synthetic patient with encounters in different sites; merge them, verify a single longitudinal view and retained aliases, then unmerge and compare all records with the pre-merge manifest.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: A demographic correction made at one site propagates to the enterprise record while retaining the prior value, source, and update time.
test_procedure: Correct a synthetic patient's name and address at one clinic; query the patient from every configured site and verify the new values, prior-value history, source facility, and timestamps.
limitation: Gap: timestamp+source via meta (source=https://vitals-vars.local/issue-35/fixtures); NO discrete Provenance/actor on a raw correction (no correction engine). Partial: not every procedure step passed; evidence capped below demonstrated.

requirement: The stack ingests, validates, and routes admit, discharge, and transfer events for all configured hospitals without changing facility or encounter identity.
authority: Industry standard (HL7 v2 ADT); IDN procurement norm
test_procedure: Replay synthetic A01, A02, A03, A08, and cancellation events from three facility senders; verify accepted and rejected messages, encounter state, facility identity, acknowledgements, and error-queue contents.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-adt-sender. Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: A cross-site transfer preserves the patient's active encounter context, medication list, allergies, orders, results, and sending and receiving locations.
test_procedure: Transfer a synthetic inpatient from Hospital A to Hospital B; compare the sending manifest with the receiving chart and verify location history, active items, discontinued items, and transfer timestamps.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-adt-sender. Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: Enterprise scheduling supports create, reschedule, cancel, and no-show operations across facility, department, provider, room, and equipment resources.
test_procedure: Create a multi-resource appointment at Clinic A, reschedule it to Clinic B, cancel it, and create a no-show; verify resource calendars, status history, notifications, and release of each reserved slot.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: An order entered at one site reaches the correct performing department and its result returns to the originating chart with unchanged patient, order, encounter, and facility references.
test_procedure: Place laboratory and imaging orders from two sites through the assembled interfaces; return synthetic results and compare identifiers and routing destinations end to end, including one deliberately misrouted result.
limitation: Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: A referral can be created, accepted, scheduled, completed, and closed across separate ambulatory organizations with status visible to both sides.
test_procedure: Send a synthetic referral from primary care to specialty care; advance it through every state, reject one invalid transition, and verify status, ownership, appointment linkage, and closure documentation in both organizations.
limitation: Gap: no referral state-machine engine exists in the slice; invalid-transition rejection is NOT domain-enforced (honest gap). Partial: not every procedure step passed; evidence capped below demonstrated.

requirement: Every clinical and administrative record identifies its owning organization, service location, source system, and responsible actor where applicable.
test_procedure: Sample patient, encounter, order, result, note, appointment, charge, and claim records created at each site; assert that organization, location, source, and actor fields are populated and resolve to configured entities.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Replaying an already accepted interface event does not create a duplicate patient, encounter, order, result, charge, or payment.
test_procedure: Submit the same synthetic ADT, order, result, charge, claim acknowledgement, and remittance payload twice; compare object counts and verify idempotent acknowledgement or a controlled duplicate rejection.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-adt-sender, simulated-payer. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: The interface layer sustains the declared four-hospital benchmark mix and drains a queued backlog without silent loss or duplication.
test_procedure: Replay a 60-minute synthetic peak mix of ADT, scheduling, order, result, charge, and claim events at increasing rates; publish sustained throughput, p95 latency, errors, backlog size, drain time, and end-to-end count reconciliation.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: A committed clinical or financial transaction remains retrievable and unchanged after a single application or database node failure.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=data durability. Commit a manifest of notes, orders, results, charges, and payments; terminate one application node and one database node in separate runs, then checksum every retrieved record against the manifest.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: After a declared downtime event, the stack restores service, reconciles queued work, and prevents duplicate or missing transactions.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=downtime recovery. Stop the clinical API and interface receiver during active synthetic workflows, queue supported transactions, restore service, and reconcile sequence, counts, acknowledgements, and user-visible state before and after recovery.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: Facility-specific departments, formularies, schedules, charge rules, and user assignments can vary without leaking configuration into another facility.
test_procedure: Configure conflicting department names, schedule templates, formulary entries, charge rules, and roles for two sites; execute identical workflows at both and verify each site applies only its own configuration.
limitation: Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: Enterprise operational reports reconcile to site-level source records for the same cutoff time and inclusion rules.
test_procedure: Generate encounter, order, result, charge, and payment totals for each site and the enterprise at a fixed synthetic cutoff; verify enterprise totals equal the site sums and trace a sample of rows back to source records.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: Each workforce user authenticates with a unique account and the stack can require multifactor authentication for privileged and remote access.
authority: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.312(a)(1), (d); ONC 45 CFR 170.315(d)(1) (Active). MFA mandate is PROPOSED only (Jan 2025 HIPAA NPRM; projected final 2027)
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=access control. Configure the identity provider for unique users and multifactor authentication; attempt local, federated, privileged, and remote sign-ins with valid, missing, expired, and replayed factors and inspect access and audit outcomes.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: Role and context rules prevent a user from reading, creating, changing, or exporting records outside the user's assigned duties and locations.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=access control. Create clinician, registrar, biller, auditor, and administrator accounts scoped to different sites; run a permission matrix across read, create, update, delete, and export actions and verify every unauthorized action is denied and logged.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Emergency break-glass access requires a reason, grants only time-limited access, and produces an immediately reviewable audit event.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=access control. Use a user without normal chart access to invoke break glass with and without a reason; verify denial when incomplete, bounded access when valid, automatic expiry, alert generation, and audit content.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: Disabling a workforce account revokes active sessions and blocks new access across clinical, administrative, API, and reporting components.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=access control. Start interactive and API sessions for a synthetic user, disable the identity-provider account, and measure revocation across every component; verify subsequent requests fail and the termination is audited.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: The stack records successful and failed authentication plus patient-record create, read, update, delete, print, export, and administrative actions.
authority: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.312(b); ONC 45 CFR 170.315(d)(2) (Active)
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=auditability. Execute each listed action through the UI and API, including denied attempts; query the audit store and match every action to user, patient where relevant, time, site, action, outcome, and source component.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Authorized auditors can search an append-only audit history by user, patient, site, action, and time without altering the underlying events.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=auditability. Generate a known event set, run each supported filter, export the report, attempt to edit and delete events through application and database roles, and verify results are complete and mutation attempts fail or trigger tamper evidence.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: The stack detects unauthorized alteration of stored clinical records and interface payloads.
authority: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.312(c)(1); ONC 45 CFR 170.315(d)(8) (Active)
test_procedure: Record hashes or signatures for a synthetic note, result, order, and inbound message; alter copies through a controlled privileged test and verify integrity checks identify each change and preserve the original evidence.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-adt-sender. Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: Protected data is encrypted in transit and at rest across application, database, object storage, queue, backup, and integration components.
authority: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.312(a)(2)(iv) and 164.312(e)(1) (encryption is ADDRESSABLE under the in-force rule; mandatory encryption is PROPOSED only, Jan 2025 NPRM)
test_procedure: Inspect configuration and negotiate connections for every component boundary; reject plaintext paths, verify certificate validation, inspect storage encryption settings, and test that snapshots and backups are unreadable without authorized keys.
limitation: Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: Secrets and encryption keys are held outside source code, scoped to the minimum component, rotated, and revoked without rebuilding the stack.
test_procedure: Scan repositories and built images for seeded test secrets, enumerate runtime secret scopes, rotate database, API, and encryption credentials, revoke old values, and verify continued service plus failed use of revoked credentials.
limitation: Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: The deployment identifies which hosting and support parties can access protected data and provides controls needed for a business associate agreement.
authority: HIPAA Security Rule business associate provisions
test_procedure: Inventory operators, subprocessors, support paths, log destinations, backups, and administrative access in the assembled deployment; map each to an owner and access control, then verify test PHI does not enter undeclared telemetry or support systems.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: Encrypted backups can restore the declared recovery point into an isolated environment with complete, readable, and internally consistent records.
authority: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.308(a)(7) (data backup, disaster recovery, and emergency mode operation all Required); IDN procurement norm
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=data durability. Create a timestamped manifest, run the documented backup, destroy an isolated test deployment, restore from backup, and reconcile patient, clinical, audit, charge, claim, and payment counts plus checksums to the declared recovery point.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: A security incident can be detected, assigned, contained, documented, and closed with preserved evidence and a complete event timeline.
test_procedure: Inject failed-login bursts, a privileged export, and a simulated compromised token; verify alert creation, assignment, containment action, evidence retention, timeline completeness, and closure approval in the configured incident workflow.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: Every deployable component has an inventory of versions and dependencies and can be scanned for known vulnerabilities before release.
test_procedure: Generate a software bill of materials for each built image and package, run the selected dependency and container scanners, verify version coverage, and demonstrate that a seeded policy-blocking finding prevents promotion.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: Development and test users, credentials, data, and interfaces cannot access the production-designated environment.
test_procedure: Create separate environment identities and synthetic datasets; attempt network, credential, queue, storage, and API access across boundaries and verify each unauthorized path is blocked and logged.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: Audit capture remains complete and queryable during the declared four-hospital peak workload and after an audit-store interruption.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=auditability. Run the synthetic peak mix while counting expected auditable actions, interrupt the audit sink, restore it, and reconcile event counts, ordering, retry behavior, search latency, and any application back-pressure.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: A clinician can view a longitudinal summary of active problems, allergies, medications, recent encounters, results, and care-team members across all IDN sites.
test_procedure: Seed one synthetic patient with records at three sites; open the summary from each site and compare every displayed item, source organization, status, and timestamp with the source manifest.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: At a transition of care, the stack can reconcile imported problems, medications, and allergies as accepted, rejected, or replaced without silently overwriting the existing lists.
test_procedure: Import a synthetic transition summary containing new, duplicate, conflicting, and discontinued items; have a clinician disposition each item and verify the resulting lists, provenance, rejected items, and reconciliation audit trail.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: A signed clinical note is immutable, and a correction creates a dated amendment linked to the original author, signer, text, and reason.
authority: IDN procurement norm (signed-note immutability and addenda); ONC 45 CFR 170.315(d)(4) Amendments (Active) for patient-requested amendments
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=auditability. Sign a synthetic note, attempt direct modification, add an amendment as the author and as another authorized clinician, and verify the original remains readable with complete authorship, reason, version, and time history.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: A laboratory order moves through placed, accepted, collected, resulted, corrected, and cancelled states without losing patient or specimen identity.
test_procedure: Place a panel, accept and collect it in the simulated laboratory, return preliminary and final results, correct one result, and cancel another; verify allowed transitions, specimen identifiers, chart display, and notifications.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: A medication order cannot be activated unless patient, drug, dose, unit, route, frequency, timing, and ordering clinician are present and valid for the configured workflow.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=medication safety. Submit complete and systematically incomplete synthetic medication orders through UI and API; verify incomplete or malformed orders cannot activate and every accepted order retains all required fields and signer identity.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: When a medication conflicts with a recorded allergy, the stack interrupts signing and requires either order revision or a documented authorized override.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=medication safety. Record coded drug-class and ingredient allergies, order matching and nonmatching drugs, and verify blocking behavior, alert content, override permissions, reason capture, and audit events.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Medication administration verifies the intended patient, active order, medication, dose, route, and time and blocks a known mismatch.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=medication safety. Simulate administrations with correct identifiers and with wrong patient, discontinued order, wrong drug, wrong dose, wrong route, and out-of-window time; verify the configured checks, blocks, overrides, and administration record.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Admission, transfer, and discharge medication reconciliation preserves the source of each medication and records continue, change, substitute, and stop decisions.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=medication safety. Seed home, ambulatory, and inpatient medication lists with conflicts; complete reconciliation at admission, transfer, and discharge and compare decisions, provenance, resulting orders, and discharge list with the expected manifest.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: An interrupted medication-order submission cannot leave a partially active, unsigned, or duplicated order.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=medication safety. Interrupt the client, API, message broker, and database connection at defined points during order signing; restore service, retry where appropriate, and verify exactly one fully signed active order or no active order.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: A result routed to a clinician can be acknowledged, reassigned, and escalated while retaining who saw it and when.
test_procedure: Return routine and abnormal synthetic results to an ordering clinician, acknowledge one, reassign one, and leave one unattended; verify inbox state, timestamps, ownership, escalation, and audit events.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: A configured critical result triggers the declared notification and escalation path until a responsible clinician acknowledges it.
test_procedure: Return a synthetic result above a configured critical threshold during staffed and after-hours periods; verify notification recipients, retry intervals, escalation sequence, acknowledgement, and closure evidence.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: Documented consent and privacy restrictions are visible at the point of use and are applied to configured disclosure and exchange workflows.
authority: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR Part 164 Subpart E); applicable consent requirements
test_procedure: Record synthetic consent states and a restricted record, then attempt routine care access, external exchange, portal release, and bulk export with permitted and nonpermitted users; verify display, enforcement, and audit outcomes.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: A clinical decision-support alert records the patient context, rule version, triggering facts, user response, and override reason when used.
authority: ONC 45 CFR 170.315(b)(11) Decision Support Interventions (Active; replaced (a)(9) CDS under HTI-1)
test_procedure: Trigger a deterministic test rule with a versioned fixture; accept, dismiss, and override it with authorized and unauthorized users, then compare displayed inputs and stored response evidence with the fixture.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: The stack can generate, transmit, receive status for, cancel, and renew a synthetic electronic prescription through a clearly identified test adapter.
authority: ONC 45 CFR 170.315(b)(3) e-prescribing (Active; updated by HTI-4, effective Oct 1 2025); NCPDP SCRIPT
test_procedure: Use the nonproduction prescribing adapter to send a new prescription, renewal, change, and cancellation; verify identifiers, status transitions, error handling, chart state, and visible labeling of the simulated external network.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: A receiving clinician can open a cross-encounter handoff that distinguishes active, completed, cancelled, and pending orders and results.
test_procedure: Create a sending encounter with mixed order and result states, transfer care to another site, and compare the receiving handoff view with the source manifest; verify unresolved work is prominent and attribution is preserved.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: The stack creates a valid synthetic eligibility inquiry and consumes the corresponding eligibility response for the same patient, payer, provider, and service date.
authority: HIPAA transaction standards, 45 CFR Part 162 Subpart L (X12 270/271 eligibility)
test_procedure: Generate a 270 fixture from registration data, submit it to the labeled test payer adapter, ingest approved, inactive, and malformed 271 fixtures, and verify correlation, parsing, status, and error handling.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer, simulated-eligibility. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Coverage details returned by eligibility are versioned and attached to the encounter used for downstream estimates and claims.
test_procedure: Return two eligibility responses with changed deductible and active dates; verify both versions, source payloads, timestamps, encounter linkage, and selection of the applicable version for a synthetic claim.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer, simulated-eligibility. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Every posted charge links to one patient, encounter, service location, performing provider, service date, code, quantity, and amount.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=claim integrity. Generate charges from ambulatory, emergency, inpatient, laboratory, and imaging workflows; reject fixtures missing each required link and trace every accepted charge back to its source event.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Coding workflow distinguishes suggested, reviewed, accepted, changed, and final codes and identifies the responsible user at each transition.
test_procedure: Seed suggested diagnosis and procedure codes for a signed synthetic encounter; accept, edit, reject, and finalize codes with different roles and verify status history, author, supporting documentation link, and audit events.
limitation: Gap: AuditEvents for raw-FHIR Claim edits=0; no coding/CDI engine emits a coding AuditEvent (version-tracked only). Partial: not every procedure step passed; evidence capped below demonstrated.

requirement: A generated institutional or professional claim preserves patient, coverage, provider, facility, service-line, diagnosis, procedure, quantity, and monetary totals from the approved source records.
authority: HIPAA transaction standards, 45 CFR Part 162 Subpart K (X12 837 claims); payer and CMS claim-content requirements (procurement norm)
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=claim integrity. Build institutional and professional synthetic claims, parse the outbound payloads, and reconcile every identifier, service line, code, quantity, and total to registration, coverage, documentation, coding, and charge records.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Pre-submission edits block a claim with missing required data, invalid internal totals, duplicate service lines, or an inactive coverage selection.
authority: Payer and CMS claim-edit requirements (procurement norm)
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=claim integrity. Submit valid fixtures plus one fixture for each listed defect; verify the valid claim advances, defective claims stop before transmission, actionable errors are shown, and corrections retain an audit trail.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Each claim submission, acknowledgement, rejection, acceptance, and payer response correlates to exactly one claim version without overwriting prior versions.
authority: X12 acknowledgment transactions (999/277CA), industry practice (not HIPAA-adopted under 45 CFR Part 162); revenue-cycle procurement norm
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=claim integrity. Submit original and corrected claim versions, return accepted, rejected, duplicate, and out-of-order acknowledgements, and verify exact correlation, immutable version history, status, and exception handling.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: An electronic remittance can be ingested and posted to the correct claim and service lines with payments, adjustments, and reason codes preserved.
authority: HIPAA transaction standards, 45 CFR Part 162 Subpart P (X12 835 remittance advice)
test_procedure: Ingest full-payment, partial-payment, denial, and malformed 835 fixtures; reconcile claim and line identifiers, payment amounts, adjustment groups, reason codes, posting status, and error-queue contents.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer, simulated-remittance. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: Contractual adjustments, patient responsibility, payer responsibility, and unapplied amounts remain separately traceable after remittance posting.
test_procedure: Post a synthetic remittance containing coinsurance, deductible, contractual adjustment, denial, and overpayment; verify ledger buckets, balances, source reason codes, and claim-to-ledger traceability.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer, simulated-remittance. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: A denied claim creates a work item with payer reason, owner, due date, source claim version, and documented resolution.
test_procedure: Return clinical, demographic, authorization, and duplicate denial fixtures; verify queue creation, categorization, assignment, due-date calculation, supporting record links, and closure requirements.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: A corrected claim resubmission preserves the original claim and denial, records each change, and prevents accidental duplicate payment posting.
authority: HIPAA transaction standards, 45 CFR Part 162 Subparts K and P (X12 837/835); revenue-cycle procurement norm
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=claim integrity. Deny an original synthetic claim, correct one field, resubmit with the required replacement reference, then post a remittance and verify immutable versions, change history, correlation, and duplicate-payment controls.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer, simulated-remittance. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: A charge, claim, payment, or adjustment can be voided or reversed only by an authorized user and without deleting the original ledger event.
test_procedure: Create and post each transaction type, attempt voids and reversals with authorized and unauthorized roles, and verify permissions, counter-entries, original-event retention, balances, and audit history.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer, simulated-remittance. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Configured-level only; full procedure not completed at this scope (preregistered not_tested).

requirement: A patient statement presents encounter dates, services, payer activity, adjustments, payments, and remaining responsibility that reconcile to the patient ledger.
test_procedure: Generate a statement from a synthetic account with two encounters, two payers, an adjustment, a payment plan, and a refund; recompute every displayed subtotal and balance from ledger entries.
limitation: Out of scope at this evidence cap; not run.

requirement: Financial record creation, modification, transmission, posting, void, reversal, and export actions are attributable to a user or system actor and time.
authority: IDN procurement norm; HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.312(b)
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=auditability. Execute each listed action through UI, API, and interface paths; reconcile the financial event ledger to the audit store and verify actor, time, source, before-and-after state, and outcome.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-payer, simulated-remittance. RCM engines run app-server-side (vmcontext sandbox has no payer egress). Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: At the declared benchmark load, claim and remittance processing finishes with no lost or duplicated service lines, claim versions, payments, or adjustments.
test_procedure: Hard-gate domain=claim integrity. Process a known synthetic batch with originals, corrected claims, denials, partial payments, reversals, and duplicate payloads at increasing rates; reconcile counts and monetary totals and publish throughput, latency, and exception results.
limitation: Preregistered hard-gate fail: out of scope at this evidence cap (load / durability / downtime / reconciliation beyond the slice).

requirement: The stack exposes a standardized FHIR R4 API for single-patient and population-level (bulk) access equivalent in scope to ONC 45 CFR 170.315(g)(10), with USCDI data classes retrievable.
authority: ONC 45 CFR 170.315(g)(10) (Active); HTI-1 USCDI v3 baseline (effective Jan 1 2026)
test_procedure: Connect a SMART on FHIR client to the stack's FHIR endpoint; retrieve a synthetic patient's USCDI data classes via the single-patient API and run a group-level bulk export; validate resource conformance and completeness against the loaded fixtures.
limitation: Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.

requirement: A patient can view, download, and transmit their health information, including visit summaries, results, and medications, through a patient-facing portal or API.
authority: IDN procurement norm; ONC 45 CFR 170.315(e)(1) View, download, and transmit to 3rd party (Active)
test_procedure: Log in as a synthetic patient proxy; view chart content, download the record export, and transmit it to a test destination; verify the export matches the chart and every access is audited.
limitation: Simulated dependencies: simulated-transmit-endpoint. Demonstrated with synthetic data; evidence packet retained.
